title: Uplifting Fluffy German Potato Dumplings
description: A monsoon-inspired German dish with fluffy potato dumplings infused with pandan leaf and a mother sauce base, all served on a heated slate tile for a rustic finish.

ingredients.name: Mother sauce base (roux of oil and flour, thickened with broth)

instructions: Peel and grate 500g potatoes, squeezing out excess moisture with a clean cloth.
In a large bowl combine grated potatoes, 100g potato starch, 50g chickpea flour, 5g baking powder, and 1 tsp salt; mix until uniform.
Tear 1 pandan leaf into small pieces and add to the potato mixture; fold gently to distribute aroma.

Make a mother sauce base by whisking 100ml vegetable broth with 100ml roux (blend 2 tbsp olive oil and 2 tbsp all‑purpose flour until smooth); heat until thickened, then pour into the potato mixture and stir until well incorporated.
Warm a slate tile in a preheated oven at 200°C for 5 minutes.
Transfer the dumpling mixture onto the heated slate and spread into a 1‑inch thick layer; bake for 25 minutes until golden and puffed.
Remove from the oven and let rest 5 minutes before cutting into squares.
Serve hot with a drizzle of fresh herbs or a light sauce if desired.
